classification_notes: Alex has 53 rows. 22 are treated as atomic events (specific process milestones, named-bidder proposals, named-bidder drops, and execution). 31 are treated as aggregate/per-party-expansion rows (15 NDA rows, 6 IOI informal bid rows for per-party detail, 8 IOI-round Drop rows for unnamed parties eliminated before Nov 3, and 2 DropTarget rows for unnamed IOI submitters cut on Nov 3).
The pipeline exports 14 rows. Two pipeline events (Longview Activist Sale, Industry Participant Bidder Interest) have no Alex atomic counterpart.
Alex's per-party NDA, per-party IOI, and per-party early-round drop rows track individual auction participants. The pipeline intentionally does not extract these at per-party granularity.

matched_events.note: Alex does not name the activist. Pipeline names JANA Partners. Both agree on date.

field_mismatches.filing_snippet: On August 13, 2014, the board ... (1) determined to explore strategic alternatives (including a possible sale of the Company)
field_mismatches.note: Aug 13 is the private board decision. The public announcement was Aug 19. Pipeline correctly labels this Target Sale. Alex mislabels it Target Sale Public.

matched_events.family: Target Sale Public / Sale Press Release

field_mismatches.filing_snippet: the Company issued a press release announcing that it determined to explore strategic alternatives
field_mismatches.note: Both labels describe the same Aug 19 press release event. Taxonomic difference only.

field_mismatches.filing_snippet: On November 3, 2014, the board met in person ... The board determined to allow the four bidders that had indicated a price or range at or above $80.00 per share to proceed to the final round
field_mismatches.note: Filing explicitly states November 3. Alex's November 15 is incorrect.

matched_events.note: Alex also records the $82.50 oral offer as a separate row (BidderID 40). Pipeline collapses to $83.00 best-and-final only.

matched_events.pipeline_actor: PetSmart board and ad hoc committee/Buyer Group

field_mismatches.note: The deal was all-cash at $83.00 per share. Pipeline does not populate all_cash on the Executed row. Alex records it. Filing supports all-cash.

matched_events.pipeline_actor: J. P. Morgan/PetSmart board and ad hoc committee

field_mismatches.filing_snippet: In July, after interviewing several potential financial advisors, the Company retained J. P. Morgan as financial advisor
field_mismatches.note: Filing says 'In July' without exact date. Pipeline records null. Alex approximates Jul 1. Both are reasonable.

pipeline_only.filing_snippet: On July 7, 2014, Longview made public a letter to the board in which Longview stated that the board should consider the a sale of the Company
pipeline_only.note: Alex has only one Activist Sale row (JANA on Jul 3). The pipeline correctly extracts Longview's separate Jul 7 activist push. Alex undercounts.

pipeline_only.filing_snippet: On August 7, 2014, a representative of Industry Participant contacted a representative of J. P. Morgan
pipeline_only.note: Alex does not track the Industry Participant interest event. Industry Participant was ultimately excluded but the interest expression is explicitly in the filing.

alex_only.filing_snippet: The Buyer Group initially submitted an oral offer of $82.50 per share, in cash
alex_only.note: The $82.50 oral offer is explicitly in the filing. Pipeline collapses to $83.00 final only. Genuine pipeline extraction gap.

alex_only.filing_snippet: Bidder 3 verbally communicated to J. P. Morgan that their valuation would not be above the current stock price of approximately $78 per share
alex_only.note: Pipeline does not export Bidder 3's verbal indication. Filing explicitly describes it.
Pipeline records Final Round Ext Ann (announcement) but not the separate deadline event. Alex captures both. Dec 12 deadline is filing-grounded.
No distinct informal final round announcement on Oct 15 in the filing. The IOI instructions were communicated during October but no specific Oct 15 event is described.
IOI deadline was Oct 30 per filing. Alex characterizes this as an informal final round. Reasonable interpretation.
Bidder 2 was not selected. Implicit drop when Buyer Group was chosen. Pipeline does not extract explicit drop events at deal close.
Bidder 3 dropped after verbal ~$78 indication on Dec 10. Alex dates final drop at Dec 14 (execution date). Filing-grounded by implication.

alex_only.filing_snippet: another bidder (which we refer to as 'Bidder 2'), which had initially indicated a price of $78.00, increased its indication to a range of $81.00 to $84.00 per share
alex_only.note: Bidder 2 revised IOI after J.P. Morgan discussions (Oct 30 - Nov 2). Pipeline does not extract informal IOI bids. Alex captures this.
Fourth final-round invitee who did not submit a competitive bid on Dec 10. Filing implies this party dropped when only Buyer Group, Bidder 2, and Bidder 3 submitted on Dec 10.

The filing describes four bidders entering the final round, but the fourth slot is occupied by Bidder 3 (two bidders working together). It is unclear whether Alex's Unnamed party 4 represents an additional entity or double-counts Bidder 3's second member. Ambiguous.

aggregate_rows.filing_snippet: the Company entered into confidentiality and standstill agreements with 15 potentially interested financial buyers
aggregate_rows.note: 15 NDA rows match the filing's 15 financial buyers exactly. Pipeline does not extract per-party NDAs. Alex names 12 as 'Unnamed party 1-12' plus Bidder 1, Bidder 2, and Buyer Group.

aggregate_rows.filing_snippet: On October 30, six of the potentially interested parties submitted indications of interest
aggregate_rows.note: Six IOI rows match the filing's six parties exactly. Named prices: Buyer Group $81-$83, another bidder $80-$85, Bidder 2 $78 initial. Three unnamed parties submitted IOIs with undisclosed prices.

aggregate_rows.filing_snippet: 15 potentially interested financial buyers ... six of the potentially interested parties submitted indications of interest
aggregate_rows.note: 15 NDA parties minus 6 IOI submitters = 9 who did not submit IOIs. Alex records 8 Drop rows for unnamed parties at the IOI stage. The 9th non-submitter may be absorbed into a later-stage row or simply not tracked. Soft match.

aggregate_rows.filing_snippet: The board determined to allow the four bidders ... to proceed to the final round
aggregate_rows.note: Six IOI submitters minus four final-round invitees = two IOI submitters eliminated on Nov 3. Alex records exactly 2 DropTarget rows. Exact count match.

field_mismatches.arbitration: Aug 13 is a private board decision. Public announcement was Aug 19. Pipeline correctly separates them.
Same Aug 19 event. Taxonomic difference only.
Filing says November 3. Alex's November 15 is incorrect.
Filing says 'In July' without exact date. Both approaches are defensible.
The deal was all-cash at $83/share. Pipeline does not populate all_cash on the Executed row. Minor omission.
Filing explicitly mentions both the $82.50 oral offer and the $83.00 final. Alex captures both. Pipeline extraction gap.

summary.overall_agreement: Pipeline and Alex agree on all major deal events with exact matches on the four formal-round proposals (date, actor, price, bid_type, all_cash). Key disagreements are limited to (1) Alex's Final Round Ann date of Nov 15 vs pipeline's filing-grounded Nov 3, (2) Alex labels Aug 13 as Target Sale Public vs pipeline's correct Target Sale, and (3) broader per-party granularity that Alex tracks but the pipeline intentionally omits.
pipeline_strengths: All 4 formal proposals match Alex exactly on date, actor, price, bid_type, and all_cash
Final Round Ann correctly dated Nov 3 per filing vs Alex's incorrect Nov 15
Target Sale correctly separated from Target Sale Public (Aug 13 private vs Aug 19 public)
Executed event matches on date and winning bidder
Longview Activist Sale and Industry Participant interest correctly extracted (Alex omits both)
pipeline_gaps: Missing Buyer Group $82.50 oral offer on Dec 12 (only captures $83.00 final)
Missing Bidder 3 verbal ~$78 indication on Dec 10
No per-party IOI informal bid events (6 parties on Oct 30)
No per-party early-round drop events (8-9 parties)
No Bidder 2 revised IOI ($81-$84) after initial $78
No Final Round Inf Ann or Final Round Inf events for the IOI round
No Final Round Ext deadline event (only captures the announcement)
No explicit drop events for Bidder 2 and Bidder 3 at deal close
all_cash not populated on Executed row
alex_errors: Final Round Ann dated Nov 15 -- filing says Nov 3
Aug 13 event labeled Target Sale Public -- this is the private board decision, not the public announcement
Final Round Inf Ann on Oct 15 has no direct filing support
summary.filing_arbitration_summary: Of 6 field mismatches: pipeline correct on 2 (Final Round Ann date, Target Sale label), Alex correct on 1 (all_cash on Executed), both valid on 2 (Sale Press Release label, intermediate $82.50 price), inconclusive on 1 (IB retention date).
